context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the same ordering convention as the headers module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: sort the keys of the map object alphabetically ascending before serializing to JSON string

context_pointers.vague_reference: apply the null-safe projection pattern defined in C017 with predicate inversion
context_pointers.actual_meaning: check if the element at index 0 is null; if true, return "empty", otherwise return the element value

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_literals.json.description: Literal parsing returns typed scalar and list values.

feature2_arithmetic_logic.json.description: Arithmetic, comparison, membership, and conditional expressions produce evaluated values.

feature3_context_variables.json.description: Context variables can be read, assigned, and reused inside one expression.

feature4_1_property_navigation.json.description: Nested properties, map entries, index aliases, and conditional property expressions resolve against a sample object graph.

feature4_2_collection_array_access.json.description: Array and collection roots expose length, iterator, indexed access, and assignment with conversion.

feature5_projection_selection.json.description: Projection and selection operators transform arrays and lists into selected result lists.

feature6_composite_construction.json.description: List, map, and array construction expressions create structured values with converted element values.

feature7_methods_static_constructors.jso.description: Expressions may invoke accessible instance behavior, static behavior, and constructors as part of evaluation.

feature8_errors.json.description: Malformed or invalid expressions are reported with normalized language-neutral error categories.

_constraints.input_condition: expression is a literal scalar (number, string, boolean, null) or list literal
_constraints.output_rule: emit kind=<enum> followed by newline, then result=<normalized_value> followed by newline
render result as integer 12345 (no decimal point)
render result as decimal 256
render result with escaped quotes: "hello world"

_constraints.input_condition: expression is list { false, true, null, 0, 1. }
_constraints.output_rule: render result as comma-separated list inside brackets, converting trailing float 1. to 1.0

_constraints.input_condition: expression contains standard arithmetic or comparison operators
_constraints.output_rule: evaluate expression respecting precedence; emit kind=number or kind=boolean and normalized result
short-circuit evaluated true branch; invalid division in false branch is ignored

_constraints.input_condition: input includes variables object and expression uses #key syntax
_constraints.output_rule: resolve #key against variables; assign and reuse variables within same expression execution

_constraints.input_condition: expression uses #five and #six declared via assignment expression
_constraints.output_rule: parse nested assignment (#five=5, 6) and resolve subsequent reference

_constraints.input_condition: fixture is 'sample' and expression accesses nested properties via [ key ] or [path]
_constraints.output_rule: return value of property; handle property names as strings or array indices
return array slice from index 0 to 2 (exclusive)

_constraints.input_condition: expression uses map array last element []
_constraints.output_rule: return last element of array (99 based on fixture context)

_constraints.input_condition: expression uses map[$]. (#this == null ? ...) to test nulls on array
_constraints.output_rule: perform conditional check where condition is true, return appropriate branch

_constraints.input_condition: fixture provides a collection root with 'size' or 'length' property
_constraints.output_rule: evaluate root property to get collection count

_constraints.input_condition: fixture provides collection root and expression is iteration hash
_constraints.output_rule: execute iterator protocol: assign to #it, call .next multiple times, call .hasNext

_constraints.input_condition: expression contains index assignment with set_to parameter
_constraints.output_rule: emit before_kind, before, after_kind, after on new line
filter collection; output is list of matching elements
filter collection to last matching element(s); output list
filter collection to only the first matching element

_constraints.input_condition: expression is a literal list {} or map {}
_constraints.output_rule: interpret braces as list or map constructor; keys/values must be quoted

_constraints.input_condition: expression is map with multiple keys # { "foo", "bar" : "baz" }
_constraints.output_rule: interpret as key-value with trailing key set to null; render map keys deterministically (sorted)
interpret as array constructor; convert element types as appropriate

_constraints.input_condition: expression contains method call (args) on fixture object
_constraints.output_rule: invoke method on instance; return result typed by method
invoke static method; result is normalized value
execute constructor, call chain behavior, return final result

_constraints.input_condition: input expression has malformed syntax (unclosed quotes, invalid escaping)
_constraints.output_rule: emit error=syntax and terminate evaluation

_constraints.input_condition: input expression has invalid variable assignment syntax
_constraints.output_rule: emit error=syntax and terminate evaluation

_constraints.input_condition: expression contains assignment operator (=) for variables
_constraints.output_rule: validate right-hand side; if valid, bind to name; if invalid, error
emit result as floating point 2.5
render result without decimal point (e.g., 11 not 11.0)
render result with decimal point (e.g., 2.5)
render result as true or false (no quotes)
render result enclosed in double quotes
render result enclosed in square brackets; comma separated; no quotes for primitives, quotes for strings/nulls
render result enclosed in square brackets with brackets
render result as integers without brackets around string representations

_constraints.input_condition: map expression has trailing key-value or multiple keys
_constraints.output_rule: render trailing keys as null value; keys in deterministic sort order
